parameters.content:
## Need more advanced automation solutions? Contact us for custom enterprise workflows!

# Growth-AI.fr

## https://www.linkedin.com/in/allanvaccarizi/
## https://www.linkedin.com/in/hugo-marinier-%F0%9F%A7%B2-6537b633/

## Trigger and email retrieval

Watches Gmail for new emails, computes today's date, and fetches the full message content to prepare data for downstream processing.

## Remove existing thread label

Strips any current processing label from the email thread so the AI agent can re-classify it cleanly.

## Fetch and filter response label

Retrieves the Gmail label that matches the AI's suggested category and filters to ensure a valid label exists before applying it.

## Apply label and mark unread

Applies the resolved label to the email thread and marks the message as unread so it surfaces for human review in Gmail.

options.systemMessage:
=You are an assistant specialized in classifying professional emails and drafting replies. ⚠️ CRITICAL FORMAT INSTRUCTION ⚠️ You must ONLY return a valid JSON object, NOTHING ELSE. - No text before the JSON - No text after the JSON - No markdown (no ```json) - No explanation - Only raw JSON Expected format: {"label": "...", "message": "..."} ## IMPORTANT CONTEXT You analyze emails that may belong to existing conversations (threads). If a thread exists, you MUST consider the full conversation context when classifying. **ABSOLUTE RULE**: You must assign ONLY ONE label per email. ## CLASSIFICATION CATEGORIES (priority order) ### 1. Urgent Requires immediate attention (< 2 hours) - Angry customer, conflict - Service down / blocking issue - Critical technical problem - Tight deadline (today/tomorrow) - Urgent business opportunity - Payment or billing issue - Keywords: "URGENT", "ASAP", "blocked", "down" ### 2. Reply Requires a response (< 48h) - Quote request - Client question - Project modification - Partner/collaborator message - Meeting request - First contact ### 3. Read Informational only - User is in CC - Group discussion not requiring input - Not directly addressed to the user - Reports, updates, newsletters ### 4. Notification Automated emails - OTP / verification codes - Platform notifications - "noreply" senders - System alerts - Confirmations ### 5. Newsletter Subscription-based content - Marketing emails - Contains "unsubscribe" ### 6. Invoice Invoice-related email - Attachments or mentions of "invoice" - Billing tools ## THREAD LOGIC If part of a thread: 1. Read ALL messages 2. Understand context 3. Adapt label: - Same context → keep label - Escalation → upgrade priority - De-escalation → downgrade If new thread: - Apply normal classification ## DRAFT WRITING ONLY for "Urgent" and "Reply" ### Tone - Match sender tone - Informal → informal - Formal → formal - Default → formal - Keep it human, concise ### Structure - Greeting - Direct answer - Clarifying questions if needed - Closing ### HTML FORMAT - Use `

` for paragraphs - Use `
` for line breaks - NO `` `` - DO NOT include a signature ### Meetings - Use calendar tool - Suggest 2–3 slots ## OUTPUT FORMAT For Notification or Read: { "label": "Notification", "message": "" } For Urgent or Reply: { "label": "Urgent", "message": "

Hello,

...

" } ## RULES 1. Only ONE label 2. Consider thread context 3. No signature 4. Adapt tone 5. Strict JSON output only 6. Only reply if email is directed to the user

options.ccList: ={{ [...(($('Fetch Email Details').item.json.to?.value || []).filter(r => r.address !== 'hugo@institut-du-referencement.com').map(r => r.address)), ...(($('Fetch Email Details').item.json.cc?.value || []).filter(r => r.address !== 'hugo@institut-du-referencement.com').map(r => r.address))].filter(Boolean).join(', ') }}

parameters.jsCode:
// Signature HTML const signature = `

HUGO MARINIER

Fondateur - Consultant spécialiste SEO / SEA

0650933800

institut-du-referencement.com

user@example.com

  

`; // Récupérer le message généré par l'agent const agentOutput = $input.first().json.output; // Si pas de message (cas Notification ou Lire), ne rien faire if (!agentOutput.message || agentOutput.message === '') { return $input.all(); } // Ajouter la signature au message const messageWithSignature = agentOutput.message + signature; // Retourner le résultat modifié return { json: { output: { Libellé: agentOutput.Libellé, message: messageWithSignature } } };

parameters.content:
## Untitled workflow

### How it works

1. A Gmail trigger detects new incoming emails and retrieves today's date and the full message content.
2. The workflow removes any existing processing label from the email thread to prepare it for a fresh AI pass.
3. An AI Agent (powered by Gemini) reads the email thread, checks the Google Calendar, and drafts a reply along with a suggested label.
4. A Switch routes the output: emails needing a draft reply are processed through a JavaScript code node that builds an HTML signature before saving a Gmail draft; other outcomes skip directly to label retrieval.
5. The workflow fetches the appropriate Gmail label for the response and applies it to the thread, marking the message as unread so it appears ready for human review.

### Setup steps

- - [ ] Connect a **Gmail OAuth2** credential to all Gmail and Gmail Trigger nodes (Gmail - Nouveaux emails, Get a message, Remove label from thread, Draft into label, Get label for response, Labelliser, Mark a message as unread).
- - [ ] Connect a **Google Gemini (PaLM)** API credential to the GEMINI sub-node inside the AI Agent.
- - [ ] Connect a **Google Calendar OAuth2** credential to the AGENDA sub-node inside the AI Agent.
- - [ ] Configure the Gmail Trigger node with the correct label/filter to watch for new emails requiring AI handling.
- - [ ] Review and update the label names used in 'Remove label from thread', 'Get label for response', and 'Labelliser' to match your Gmail label setup.
- - [ ] Customize the HTML signature in the 'Code in JavaScript' node to match your desired email signature.
- - [ ] Review the Switch1 routing conditions to ensure they match the AI Agent's output structure from the PARCER (structured output parser).

### Customization

You can adjust the AI Agent's system prompt to change the tone or structure of drafted replies. The Switch1 conditions can be modified to add more routing branches (e.g., forward, archive, escalate). The HTML signature in the Code in JavaScript node can be updated with your personal or company branding.

## AI agent drafting and routing

An AI Agent (Gemini LLM) reads the email thread and calendar, then drafts a reply and assigns a label category. A structured output parser ensures clean output. A Switch node routes the result to either draft creation or direct label assignment based on the AI's decision.

## Build and save Gmail draft

Generates an HTML email signature via JavaScript code, then saves the AI-composed reply as a Gmail draft associated with the original thread.
